title: AI-Based Cross-Layer Vulnerability Management for Cloud-Native Systems
authors: Oleh Polishchuk, Kateryna Babii
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on AI in Cybersecurity (ICAIC)
abstract: Cloud-native systems introduce complex and dynamic attack surfaces driven by microservice architectures, identity-centric access models, and continuously evolving runtime behavior. Traditional vulnerability management approaches rely on static severity scoring and siloed security signals, resulting in excessive alert noise and limited insight into real-world exploitability.This paper presents a novel AI-driven cross-layer vulnerability management framework that models exploitation risk as an evolving behavioral process rather than a static property of individual vulnerabilities. The proposed approach represents cloud-native environments as temporal cross-layer graphs and applies self-supervised sequence learning, graph-based risk propagation, and anomaly detection to identify multi-stage exploitation paths spanning application, identity, runtime, and endpoint layers.Unlike conventional prioritization techniques, the framework learns how risk propagates across layers over time and adapts to previously unseen attack strategies without reliance on labeled exploit data. A simulated Kubernetes-based case study demonstrates substantial alert noise reduction while preserving exploitation-relevant signals, outperforming severity-based and rule-based correlation approaches.
title_zh: 基于人工智能的云原生系统跨层漏洞管理
abstract_zh: 云原生系统由于微服务架构、以身份为中心的访问模型以及持续演化的运行时行为，引入了复杂且动态的攻击面。传统的漏洞管理方法依赖静态严重性评分和孤立的安全信号，导致告警噪声过多，且难以洞察实际的可利用性。本文提出了一种新型的AI驱动跨层漏洞管理框架，将利用风险建模为一个动态演变的行为过程，而非单一漏洞的静态属性。该方法将云原生环境表示为时序跨层图，结合自监督序列学习、基于图的风险传播以及异常检测技术，识别跨越应用层、身份层、运行时层和终端层的多阶段利用路径。与传统优先级排序技术不同，该框架能够学习风险随时间在各层间的传播规律，并在无需依赖标注的利用数据的情况下，适应此前未见的攻击策略。一项基于Kubernetes的模拟案例研究显示，该方法显著减少了告警噪声，同时保留了与实际利用相关的信号，性能优于基于严重性评分和规则关联的传统方法。

title: Tawa: Automatic Warp Specialization for Modern GPUs with Asynchronous References
authors: Hongzheng Chen, Bin Fan, Alexander Collins, Bastian Hagedorn, Evghenii Gaburov, Masahiro Masuda, Matthew Brookhart, Chris Sullivan, Jason Knight, Zhiru Zhang, Vinod Grover
publish: 2026 IEEE/ACM International Symposium on Code Generation and Optimization (CGO)
abstract: Modern GPUs feature specialized hardware units that enable high-performance, asynchronous dataflow execution. However, the conventional SIMT programming model is fundamentally misaligned with this task-parallel hardware, creating a significant programmability gap. While hardware-level warp specialization is the key to unlocking peak performance, it forces developers to manually orchestrate complex, low-level communication and software pipelines—a process that is labor-intensive, error-prone, and unsustainable. To address this challenge, we present Tawa, an automated compiler that systematically generates high-performance, warp-specialized code from a high-level, tile-based program. Central to our approach is a novel IR abstraction, asynchronous references (aref), which expresses warp-level communication without exposing low-level hardware details. Using this abstraction, Tawa automatically partitions programs into producer-consumer roles and manages the intricate dataflow pipeline, relieving developers of invasive kernel rewriting. Evaluation on NVIDIA H100 GPUs across representative LLM kernels shows that Tawa delivers high hardware utilization, achieving up to 1.1 × speedup over highly optimized cuBLAS GEMM kernels. For attention workloads, Tawa attains 1.2× speedup over Triton and matches the performance of the hand-optimized CUTLASS C++ FlashAttention-3 kernel with far less programming effort.
title_zh: Tawa：面向现代GPU的异步引用自动扭曲专业化
abstract_zh: 现代GPU配备了专用的硬件单元，支持高性能的异步数据流执行。然而，传统的SIMT编程模型与这种任务并行的硬件架构在本质上存在不匹配，造成了显著的可编程性鸿沟。尽管在硬件层面实现线程束（warp）专用化是实现峰值性能的关键，但这迫使开发者手动协调复杂的低级通信和软件流水线，这一过程既耗时又容易出错，且难以持续维护。为解决这一挑战，我们提出了Tawa——一种自动编译器，能够从高层、基于分块（tile-based）的程序中系统性地生成高性能的线程束专用代码。我们方法的核心是一种新颖的中间表示（IR）抽象——异步引用（asynchronous references, aref），它能够在不暴露底层硬件细节的前提下，表达线程束级别的通信。借助这一抽象，Tawa能够自动将程序划分为生产者-消费者角色，并管理复杂的数据流流水线，从而免除开发者对内核进行侵入式重写的负担。在NVIDIA H100 GPU上对代表性大语言模型（LLM）内核的评估表明，Tawa实现了高硬件利用率，相较于高度优化的cuBLAS GEMM内核最高可提升1.1倍性能。在注意力计算负载下，Tawa相比Triton实现1.2倍的加速，并且在远少的编程工作量下，达到了手工优化的CUTLASS C++ FlashAttention-3内核的性能水平。

title: Integrating AI into DevSecOps Pipelines for Secure Cloud Microservices Management
authors: Atish Kumar Dash
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on AI in Cybersecurity (ICAIC)
abstract: The modern era of technology encompasses many innovative areas such as cloud computing and the applications and services within them. These workloads, primarily based on microservices architectures, have ushered in blazing fast applications and cloud services. The entire lifecycle of these workloads embedded within traditional DevOps model has been redefined with a shift left approach to security. This is where DevSecOps promises to be a major game changer in this context. This paper explores the overall merger between such cloud workloads and artificial intelligence (AI). While such a combination is pretty much in its nascent stage, this study delves deeper into ways in which such integrations can work seamlessly across stakeholders. The issues of containerized workloads across various pipeline stages, including code commit, build, testing, deployment, and runtime monitoring are investigated and given adequate attention. The paper also explores the trade-offs involved in meeting exponential consumer demand with improved security and threat surface minimization of these cloud-based microservices inclusive of real-world examples. The current AI-driven security automation processes are examined and the limitations of such static rule based, and reactive models are also touched upon. AI-driven techniques such as anomaly detection, predictive risk scoring, intelligent vulnerability prioritization, and automated policy enforcement are further looked at from a regulatory perspective for large enterprise environments. AI-driven DevSecOps provides a step forward in enhancing the security and reliability of cloud-native applications. The present study further calls out AI explainability and real-world validation, specifically from a microservices architecture standpoint and how to manage them effectively to accelerate industry wide adoption.
title_zh: 将人工智能融入DevSecOps流水线以实现安全的云微服务管理
abstract_zh: 现代技术时代涵盖了众多创新领域，如云计算及其内部的应用与服务。这些工作负载主要基于微服务架构，推动了极速响应的应用程序和云服务的诞生。在传统的DevOps模式下，这些工作负载的整个生命周期已通过“左移”安全理念得到重新定义，而DevSecOps在此背景下有望成为重大变革力量。本文探讨了云工作负载与人工智能（AI）融合的总体趋势。尽管这种结合尚处于起步阶段，但本研究深入分析了如何在各利益相关方之间实现无缝集成。文章重点研究了容器化工作负载在各个流水线阶段（包括代码提交、构建、测试、部署及运行时监控）所面临的问题，并给予充分关注。同时，本文还探讨了在满足指数级消费者需求的同时，如何在提升安全性、缩小攻击面方面实现权衡，并结合现实世界案例进行说明。当前基于AI的安全自动化流程被深入分析，同时指出了静态规则驱动和被动响应模型的局限性。从大型企业环境的监管视角出发，进一步探讨了AI驱动的异常检测、预测性风险评分、智能漏洞优先级排序以及自动化策略执行等技术。AI驱动的DevSecOps为提升云原生应用的安全性与可靠性迈出了重要一步。本研究还特别强调了AI可解释性与现实世界验证的重要性，尤其是从微服务架构的角度出发，探讨了如何有效管理这些挑战，以加速行业范围内的广泛采纳。

title: AI in Cybersecurity: Fighting Hackers With Smarter Technologies
authors: Jampana Ruthvika, Utkarsha Pacharaney, Ankita Deorao Tijare, Palak Nandane, Sai Praveena Nulu, Gaurav Mahakalkar
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: As cyber threats evolve in complexity and scale, con-ventional security measures increasingly fall short in effectively defending digital assets. Artificial intelligence (AI) has emerged as a powerful ally in cybersecurity, offering advanced capabilities that enhance threat detection, automate incident response, and enable proactive defense strategies. This article examines the pivotal role of AI technologies-such as machine learning, deep learning, and behavioral analytics-in strengthening cyberse-curity systems against modern attacks. It highlights key AI-driven methodologies employed to detect anomalies, predict emerging threats, and respond swiftly, while also addressing the challenges and ethical considerations in implementing AI-based security solutions. The discussion extends to future directions where autonomous AI agents and explainable AI can further revolutionize the fight against cyber adversaries. Overall, this work underscores AI's transformative impact as a smarter technological force in safeguarding against hackers.
title_zh: 人工智能在网络安全中的应用：用更智能的技术对抗黑客
abstract_zh: 随着网络威胁在复杂性和规模上的不断演变，传统的安全措施在有效保护数字资产方面日益显得力不从心。人工智能（AI）已成为网络安全领域的重要助力，凭借其先进的能力，显著提升了威胁检测水平，实现了事件响应的自动化，并推动了主动防御策略的发展。本文探讨了人工智能技术——如机器学习、深度学习和行为分析——在增强网络安全系统以应对现代攻击中的关键作用。文章重点介绍了基于AI的关键方法，用于识别异常行为、预测新兴威胁并实现快速响应，同时分析了在实施AI安全解决方案过程中面临的挑战与伦理考量。此外，文章还展望了未来发展方向，指出自主AI代理和可解释AI有望进一步革新对抗网络攻击的格局。总体而言，本文强调了人工智能作为更智能的技术力量，在抵御黑客攻击方面所具有的变革性影响。

title: An Explainable Machine Learning Framework for Predicting Software Defects in Large-Scale Software Systems
authors: Srikanth Kavuri
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on AI in Cybersecurity (ICAIC)
abstract: Software-intensive systems are rapidly growing in size and complexity, and early defect prediction is very important in enhancing reliability and minimizing the costs of maintenance. Traditional defect prediction models focus on performance but offer limited transparency, thereby hampering their application. This research introduces a transparent ML model for large-scale software defect prediction using the JM1 dataset. The framework also includes preprocessing systematic data, addressing class imbalance using SMOTE, and scaling features to improve learning efficiency. An Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) model is used to model complex, non-linear associations among software metrics. By combining local and global explanations of a prediction, explainable AI systems such as LIME and SHAP address the interpretability gap in black-box models. Experimental testing shows that the suggested method achieves 96.65% recall, 96.65% accuracy, and 96.65% F1-score. The findings indicate that the framework offers high predictability and transparency, supporting informed decision-making in software quality assurance.
title_zh: 一种用于预测大规模软件系统中软件缺陷的可解释机器学习框架
abstract_zh: 软件密集型系统正迅速向更大规模和更复杂的方向发展，早期缺陷预测在提升系统可靠性并降低维护成本方面具有重要意义。传统缺陷预测模型虽然注重性能，但可解释性较差，限制了其实际应用。本研究提出一种基于JM1数据集的透明化机器学习模型，用于大规模软件缺陷预测。该框架包括系统化的数据预处理、采用SMOTE方法解决类别不平衡问题，以及特征缩放以提高学习效率。通过使用极端梯度提升（XGBoost）模型，有效捕捉软件度量指标之间的复杂非线性关系。结合局部与全局解释方法，可解释人工智能技术如LIME和SHAP，弥补了黑箱模型在可解释性方面的不足。实验结果表明，所提出的方法在召回率、准确率和F1分数上均达到96.65%。研究结果表明，该框架不仅具备高预测能力，还具有良好的透明性，能够为软件质量保证中的决策提供有力支持。

title: Proton: Towards Multi-level, Adaptive Profiling for Triton
authors: Keren Zhou, Tianle Zhong, Hao Wu, Jihyeong Lee, Yue Guan, Yufei Ding, Corbin Robeck, Yuanwei Fang, Jeff Niu, Philippe Tillet
publish: 2026 IEEE/ACM International Symposium on Code Generation and Optimization (CGO)
abstract: Domain-Specific languages (DSLs) such as Triton enable developers to write high-performance GPU kernels in a Python-friendly manner; however, profiling these kernels with existing tools often incurs runtime and storage overhead while failing to deliver actionable insights for both kernel authors and framework developers. We present Proton, a multi-level, adaptive profiler tailored for the Triton programming language and compiler. Proton provides frontend APIs to selectively profile relevant regions, aggregate results, capture custom metrics not available through hardware counters, and query profiles using a SQL-like language. Proton’s backend design unifies vendor profiling APIs with instrumentation-based profiling, ensuring portability and extensibility. Using Proton, users are able to query custom and hardware metrics across the relevant levels of abstraction—full end-to-end model execution, isolated neural network layers, language-specific Triton operators, and compiler intermediate representations. We demonstrate the tool’s effectiveness through case studies on production-grade kernel development, continuous integration, multi-GPU analysis, language model inference, and intra-kernel profiling. Our evaluations on end-to-end workloads, as well as standalone Triton kernels, demonstrate that Proton imposes lower runtime overhead and delivers significant reductions in profile sizes relative to existing framework and vendor profilers while being fully open source.
title_zh: 质子：面向Triton的多层次、自适应性能分析
abstract_zh: 领域特定语言（DSL）如Triton使开发者能够以Python友好的方式编写高性能GPU内核；然而，使用现有工具对这些内核进行性能分析时，通常会带来显著的运行时开销和存储开销，且难以提供对内核开发者和框架开发者具有实际指导意义的洞察。为此，我们提出了Proton——一种专为Triton编程语言和编译器设计的多层次、自适应性能分析工具。Proton提供了前端API，支持选择性地分析关键代码区域，聚合分析结果，捕获硬件计数器无法提供的自定义指标，并通过类似SQL的语言查询性能数据。Proton的后端设计将厂商提供的性能分析API与基于插桩的分析方法统一起来，确保了良好的可移植性和可扩展性。借助Proton，用户可以在多个抽象层次上查询自定义指标和硬件指标，包括完整的端到端模型执行、独立的神经网络层、语言特定的Triton操作符，以及编译器中间表示（IR）。我们通过多个实际案例展示了该工具的有效性，涵盖生产级内核开发、持续集成、多GPU分析、语言模型推理以及内核内部的细粒度性能分析。在端到端工作负载和独立Triton内核上的评估表明，Proton相比现有的框架级和厂商级分析工具，不仅显著降低了运行时开销，大幅减少了性能分析数据的存储体积，而且完全开源。

title: Semi-Supervised and Transfer Learning-Based Smart Contract Vulnerability Detection
authors: Hengjie Song, Yangkai Wang, Han Yu, Le Ma, Siyu Jiang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Smart contracts are crucial for managing sensitive financial transactions. However, these contracts are inherently vulnerable which can cause significant security risks. Traditional methods for detecting smart contract vulnerabilities depend on expert-defined rules, which are often complicated and limited by human experts' individual experience. In contrast, deep learning-based approaches automatically extract intricate feature representations, greatly improving detection efficiency and accuracy. Nevertheless, these approaches rely on access to large amounts of high-quality labeled data. They are less effective when facing new types of vulnerabilities where labeled data are scarce. To this end, we propose the Semi-supervised Tuning (SST) approach for smart contract vulnerability detection. It first leverages a source model trained on labeled source data to extract features for new vulnerabilities. Subsequently, it performs semi-supervised learning to explore the feature structure of unlabeled data. In particular, SST groups contract code features and constructs a shared feature queue containing labeled and unlabeled contracts to explore the complete feature structure and guide model training. Extensive experimental evaluations based on two real-world datasets containing eleven smart contract vulnerabilities demonstrate that SST is significantly more advantageous compared to eight state-of-the-art baseline methods, outperforming them by 24.12% in terms of F1 scores.
title_zh: 基于半监督与迁移学习的智能合约漏洞检测
abstract_zh: 智能合约在管理敏感金融交易方面至关重要。然而，这些合约本身存在固有漏洞，可能引发严重的安全风险。传统的漏洞检测方法依赖于专家定义的规则，往往复杂且受限于人类专家的个人经验。相比之下，基于深度学习的方法能够自动提取复杂的特征表示，显著提升了检测的效率和准确性。然而，这些方法通常需要大量高质量的标注数据，当面对新型漏洞且标注数据稀缺时，其效果会大打折扣。为此，我们提出了一种半监督调优（Semi-supervised Tuning, SST）方法，用于智能合约漏洞检测。该方法首先利用在标注源数据上训练的源模型，提取新型漏洞的特征；随后通过半监督学习探索未标注数据的特征结构。具体而言，SST对合约代码特征进行分组，并构建一个包含标注与未标注合约的共享特征队列，以全面探索特征结构并指导模型训练。基于包含十一种智能合约漏洞的两个真实世界数据集的大量实验评估表明，SST显著优于八种最先进的基线方法，在F1分数上领先达24.12%。

title: Pyls: Enabling Python Hardware Synthesis with Dynamic Polymorphism via LCRS Encoding
authors: Bolei Tong, Yongyan Fang, Chaorui Wang, Qingan Li, Jingling Xue, Mengting Yuan
publish: 2026 IEEE/ACM International Symposium on Code Generation and Optimization (CGO)
abstract: Python dominates AI development and is the most widely used dynamic programming language, but synthesizing its polymorphic functions into hardware remains challenging. Existing HLS solutions support only static subsets of Python, forcing CPU offload with costly communication overhead. We present Pyls, the first framework that synthesizes dynamically polymorphic Python into monolithic hardware via Left-Child Right-Sibling (LCRS) encoding. Key to our approach is representing all Python objects as LCRS trees, enabling uniform hardware handling of dynamic types. Pyls automatically converts objects to fixed-width formats, generates XLS IR designs, and implements a tree memory architecture for efficient runtime type resolution. On FPGA platforms, Pyls demonstrates speedups of 5.19× and 3.98× over two ASIC CPUs, 303.29× over a soft-core processor, and 282.66× over a heterogeneous SoC design.
title_zh: Pyls：通过LCRS编码实现动态多态性的Python硬件合成
abstract_zh: Python在人工智能开发中占据主导地位，是使用最广泛的动态编程语言，但将其多态函数合成到硬件仍面临挑战。现有的高层次综合（HLS）解决方案仅支持Python的静态子集，导致必须将计算任务卸载到CPU，带来高昂的通信开销。我们提出了Pyls，这是首个通过左子右兄弟（LCRS）编码将动态多态Python代码合成到单一硬件的框架。我们方法的核心在于将所有Python对象表示为LCRS树结构，从而实现对动态类型的统一硬件处理。Pyls能够自动将对象转换为定宽格式，生成XLS中间表示（IR）设计，并实现一种树形内存架构，以高效完成运行时类型解析。在FPGA平台上，Pyls相较于两种ASIC CPU分别实现了5.19倍和3.98倍的加速，相较于软核处理器提升了303.29倍，相较于异构SoC设计提升了282.66倍。

title: Model as a Game: On Numerical and Spatial Consistency for Generative Games
authors: Jingye Chen, Yuzhong Zhao, Yupan Huang, Lei Cui, Li Dong, Tengchao Lv, Qifeng Chen, Furu Wei
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Recent advances in generative models have significantly impacted game generation. However, despite producing high-quality graphics and adequately receiving player input, existing models often fail to maintain fundamental game properties such as numerical and spatial consistency. Numerical consistency ensures gameplay mechanics correctly reflect score changes and other quantitative elements, while spatial consistency prevents jarring scene transitions, providing seamless player experiences. In this paper, we revisit the paradigm of generative games to explore what truly constitutes a Model as a Game (MaaG) with a well-developed mechanism. We begin with an empirical study on “Traveler”, a 2D game created by an LLM featuring minimalist rules yet challenging generative models in maintaining consistency. Based on the DiT architecture, we design two specialized modules: (1) a numerical module that integrates a LogicNet to determine event triggers, with calculations processed externally as conditions for image generation; and (2) a spatial module that maintains a map of explored areas, retrieving location-specific information during generation and linking new observations to ensure continuity. Experiments across three games demonstrate that our integrated modules significantly enhance performance on consistency metrics compared to baselines, while incurring minimal time overhead during inference. We will release our code and models to facilitate future research in this area, with code and samples in the supplementary material.
title_zh: 作为游戏的模型：生成游戏中的数值与空间一致性
abstract_zh: 生成模型的最新进展已对游戏生成领域产生了深远影响。然而，尽管现有模型能够生成高质量的图形并合理响应玩家输入，它们在维持游戏的基本属性方面仍存在明显不足，例如数值一致性和空间一致性。数值一致性确保游戏机制能正确反映分数变化等量化要素，而空间一致性则避免场景切换时出现突兀感，从而提供流畅的玩家体验。本文重新审视生成式游戏的范式，探讨何为具备完善机制的“模型即游戏”（Model as a Game, MaaG）。我们以由大语言模型（LLM）生成的2D游戏“Traveler”为研究对象，该游戏规则极简，却对生成模型在保持一致性方面提出了严峻挑战。基于DiT架构，我们设计了两个专用模块：（1）数值模块，集成LogicNet以判断事件触发条件，并将计算结果作为图像生成的外部条件；（2）空间模块，维护已探索区域的地图，生成过程中检索位置相关的信息，并通过关联新观察结果来保证场景连续性。在三个游戏上的实验表明，与基线方法相比，我们的集成模块显著提升了各项一致性指标的表现，同时在推理阶段仅带来极小的时间开销。我们将在补充材料中公开代码与模型，以推动该领域的后续研究。

title: DFT AI: Machine Learning-Guided Test Point Insertion for Pre-Silicon Debug and Post-Silicon Diagnosis in Complex VLSI Systems
authors: Deepika Bhatia
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on AI in Cybersecurity (ICAIC)
abstract: The rising complexity of modern System-on-Chip (SoC) designs has made it increasingly important to deploy intelligent Design-for-Testability (DFT) strategies that support both pre-silicon debug and post-silicon fault diagnosis. Conventional test point insertion (TPI) techniques are typically driven by static heuristics or ATPG-based analyses, and often struggle to scale in the presence of diverse microarchitectures, tight timing budgets, and strict area constraints. This paper introduces DFT AI, a machine learning–guided framework that automates TPI using data-driven reasoning and structural learning. The approach combines gradient-boosted decision trees with graph neural networks (GNNs) to estimate signal importance from structural, dynamic, and contextual features extracted from synthesized RTL designs. A constraint-aware selector ensures that only high-impact, timing-safe test points are chosen. We evaluate DFT AI on ITC’99 and OpenCores benchmark suites and observe up to 28% improvement in observability gain and a 27% reduction in diagnostic ambiguity over state-of-the-art methods, with modest area penalty and significantly reduced runtime. The framework generalizes well across heterogeneous designs and provides interpretability via SHAP-based signal analysis, offering a scalable and trustworthy solution for lifecycle-wide DFT automation.
title_zh: DFT AI：基于机器学习的测试点插入技术，用于复杂VLSI系统的硅前调试与硅后诊断
abstract_zh: 现代片上系统（SoC）设计的复杂性日益增加，使得部署能够支持硅前调试与硅后故障诊断的智能设计可测性（DFT）策略变得愈发重要。传统的测试点插入（TPI）技术通常依赖静态启发式方法或基于ATPG的分析，在面对多样化的微架构、严格的时序预算以及苛刻的面积约束时，往往难以实现有效扩展。本文提出DFT AI，一种基于机器学习引导的框架，通过数据驱动推理与结构化学习实现TPI的自动化。该方法结合梯度提升决策树与图神经网络（GNN），从综合后的RTL设计中提取的结构、动态及上下文特征出发，评估信号的重要性。一个考虑约束条件的筛选器确保仅选择高影响且时序安全的测试点。我们在ITC’99和OpenCores基准测试集上对DFT AI进行了评估，结果表明，相较于现有最先进方法，其可观测性提升最高达28%，诊断模糊性降低27%，同时仅带来适度的面积开销，并显著缩短运行时间。该框架在异构设计间具有良好泛化能力，并通过基于SHAP的信号分析提供可解释性，为全生命周期DFT自动化提供了一种可扩展且可信的解决方案。

title: Codebase Aware Generative Agents for the SDLC: Automating Documentation, Dependency Analysis and Test Generation
authors: Vatsal Kishorbhai Mavani
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on AI in Cybersecurity (ICAIC)
abstract: Autonomous agents are becoming common in software development, but they often struggle to maintain context in large, changing codebases. To solve this, we propose CA-SAF (Codebase-Aware Self-Adapting Framework), a multi-agent system designed to fix this architectural "blindness" by actively retrieving precise code details. Our core innovation is ZSDM (Zero-Shot Dependency Mapping). This hybrid method combines AST parsing with strict LLM extraction to map every entity and relationship into a queryable Knowledge Graph. By using structured code summaries, we achieved roughly 80% data compression, ensuring the context remains efficient. We benchmarked the system against GPT-4o, LLaMA 3.1, and Claude 3 Opus. We found that Claude 3 Opus, with its 200,000 - token window, slightly outperformed the others (capped at 128,000) on complex, non-local mapping tasks. Over a six-month operational study, using CA-SAF led to a measurable drop in both Cycle Time and Lead Time for maintenance work. Crucially, the framework used this codebase awareness to balance speed with quality, actively reducing the number of critical bugs that high-speed LLM generation often introduces.
title_zh: 面向代码库的生成式智能体在软件开发生命周期中的应用：自动化文档生成、依赖分析与测试用例生成
abstract_zh: 自主代理在软件开发中正变得越来越普遍，但它们在大型且不断变化的代码库中往往难以维持上下文。为解决这一问题，我们提出了CA-SAF（代码库感知自适应框架），这是一个多代理系统，旨在通过主动检索精确的代码细节来克服这种架构上的“盲区”。我们的核心创新是ZSDM（零样本依赖映射）技术。该混合方法结合了抽象语法树（AST）解析与严格的大型语言模型（LLM）提取，将每个代码实体及其关系映射为可查询的知识图谱。通过使用结构化的代码摘要，我们实现了约80%的数据压缩，确保上下文信息保持高效。我们在GPT-4o、LLaMA 3.1和Claude 3 Opus之间进行了基准测试，发现Claude 3 Opus凭借其20万token的上下文窗口（其他模型上限为12.8万），在复杂的非局部映射任务中表现略胜一筹。在为期六个月的运行研究中，采用CA-SAF显著降低了维护工作的周期时间（Cycle Time）和交付前置时间（Lead Time）。尤为重要的是，该框架利用对代码库的感知能力，在速度与质量之间实现了良好平衡，有效减少了高速LLM生成常带来的关键缺陷数量。

title: Tensor Program Superoptimization through Cost-Guided Symbolic Program Synthesis
authors: Alexander Brauckmann, Aarsh Chaube, José W. De Souza Magalhães, Elizabeth Polgreen, Michael F. P. O’Boyle
publish: 2026 IEEE/ACM International Symposium on Code Generation and Optimization (CGO)
abstract: Modern tensor compiler frameworks like JAX and PyTorch accelerate numerical programs by compiling or mapping Domain-Specific Language (DSL) code to efficient executables. However, they rely on a fixed set of transformation rules and heuristics, which means they can miss profitable optimization opportunities. This leaves significant optimization potential unused for programs that fall outside these fixed patterns.This paper presents STENSO, a tensor DSL program superoptimizer that discovers such missing rewrites. STENSO’s core is a symbolic program synthesis based search algorithm that systematically explores the space of equivalent programs. By combining symbolic execution and sketch-based program synthesis, it generates equivalent candidate implementations. To make the search computationally tractable, STENSO further integrates a cost model with a branch-and-bound algorithm scheme. This effectively prunes the search space, arriving at optimal solutions in a reasonable time.We evaluate STENSO on over 30 benchmarks. The discovered programs achieve geometric mean speedups of 3.8x over NumPy and 1.6x over state-of-the-art compilers like JAX and PyTorch-Inductor. These results underscore the limitations of heuristic-based compilation and demonstrate STENSO’s effectiveness in finding such optimizations automatically.
title_zh: 通过成本引导的符号程序合成实现张量程序超优化
abstract_zh:
像JAX和PyTorch这样的现代张量编译框架通过将领域特定语言（DSL）代码编译或映射为高效可执行程序来加速数值计算。然而，这些框架依赖于一组固定的变换规则和启发式方法，因此可能错失一些有收益的优化机会。对于那些不符合这些固定模式的程序而言，这种局限性导致大量优化潜力未被利用。

本文提出了STENSO，一种用于张量DSL程序的超优化器，能够发现这些被遗漏的代码重写机会。STENSO的核心是一种基于符号程序合成的搜索算法，系统地探索等价程序的空间。通过结合符号执行与基于程序模板（sketch-based）的程序合成技术，STENSO能够生成等价的候选实现。为了使搜索在计算上可行，STENSO进一步引入了一个成本模型，并采用分支定界（branch-and-bound）算法框架，有效剪枝搜索空间，在合理时间内找到最优解。

我们在超过30个基准测试上评估了STENSO。所发现的程序相比NumPy实现了3.8倍的几何平均加速，相比JAX和PyTorch-Inductor等最先进的编译器也达到了1.6倍的加速。这些结果凸显了基于启发式方法的编译技术的局限性，同时证明了STENSO能够自动发现此类优化的高效性。

title: LLM-Powered Autonomous Security Agents for Next-Generation Cyber Defence
authors: Tanvir H. Sardar, Bishwajeet Pandey, Laura Aldasheva
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on AI in Cybersecurity (ICAIC)
abstract: The paper proposes a new system design of rolling out Large Language Model (LLM)-driven autonomous security agents in enterprise cybersecurity. Cyber threats are becoming more complex and security operations centres (SOCs) are being overwhelmed by alerts, which is why traditional Security Orchestration, Automation, and Response (SOAR) systems based on those originally relying on rules are no longer able to cope with new attack patterns. We introduce SENTINEL (Security-Enabled Neural Threat Intelligence and Learning), an architecture, which uses LLMs as the reasoning engine to handle autonomous threat investigation, incident triage, and coordinated autonomous response. Our framework deals with such issues as reliability of agents, resistance to adversarial manipulation, and reasonable limits of human control. A three-environment experimental assessment shows that SENTINEL can be used to classify threats with an accuracy of 94.2% and is able to minimize the mean time to respond (MTTR) by 73-percent and detect 89-percent of new attack measures that could not be detected by traditional systems. We also present a formal verification scheme on how to ensure that agent behavior does not exceed specified safety limits so as to allow reliable autonomous behavior in the production security setting.
title_zh: 基于大语言模型的自主安全智能体：下一代网络防御新范式
abstract_zh: 本文提出了一种在企业网络安全领域部署大型语言模型（LLM）驱动的自主安全代理的新系统设计。随着网络威胁日益复杂，安全运营中心（SOC）正面临海量告警信息的冲击，传统基于规则的自动化响应系统（SOAR）已难以应对新型攻击模式。为此，我们提出了SENTINEL（安全增强型神经威胁情报与学习架构），该架构以大型语言模型作为推理引擎，实现自主威胁调查、事件优先级排序及协同自主响应。我们的框架有效解决了代理可靠性、抗对抗性操纵能力以及人类控制合理边界等关键问题。通过三环境实验评估，结果表明，SENTINEL在威胁分类任务中准确率达到94.2%，可将平均响应时间（MTTR）降低73%，并能检测出传统系统无法识别的89%的新攻击手段。此外，我们还提出了一种形式化验证方案，确保代理行为始终在预设的安全边界内运行，从而保障在生产环境中的可靠自主运作。

title: Multi-Layered Security Framework for Financial AI Solutions - PISA
authors: Aruun K. Kumar, George Belsian, Santosh Srinivasan, Giridhar Shyam Sankararaman
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on AI in Cybersecurity (ICAIC)
abstract: Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI) is rapidly transforming organizations, enabling intelligent customer interactions, automated analysis, and agentic decision workflows. At the same time, GenAI introduces a new class of probabilistic and semantic vulnerabilities like prompt manipulation, hallucinations, sensitive-information leakage, and unauthorized agentic actions, that traditional cybersecurity controls are not designed to mitigate. These risks are particularly acute in financial services, a sector widely recognized as part of national critical infrastructure, where security failures can trigger immediate financial loss, regulatory non-compliance, and systemic instability. This paper introduces PISA, a four-layer security framework designed specifically to protect GenAI applications in financial institutions. PISA integrates prompt-layer defense, information-boundary controls, semantic verification, and agent-authorization safeguards into a unified defense-in-depth model. We present a reference architecture implementing PISA, along with an adversarial evaluation across high-risk financial use cases. The results demonstrate substantial reductions in prompt-injection success, hallucination rates, and unauthorized action attempts compared to baseline GenAI systems. This research provides financial institutions with a practical and sector-aligned methodology for deploying secure, trustworthy, and regulation-aware GenAI systems.
title_zh: 金融人工智能解决方案的多层安全框架——PISA
abstract_zh: 生成式人工智能（GenAI）正在迅速重塑各类组织，推动智能客户互动、自动化分析以及代理式决策工作流的发展。然而，GenAI也引入了一类新型的概率性与语义性安全漏洞，例如提示词操纵、幻觉现象、敏感信息泄露以及未经授权的代理行为，而传统网络安全防护措施并未针对此类风险进行设计。在金融服务业，这类风险尤为突出，该行业被广泛视为国家关键基础设施的重要组成部分，安全漏洞可能导致即时财务损失、监管违规以及系统性不稳定。本文提出PISA——一种专为保护金融机构中GenAI应用而设计的四层安全框架。PISA将提示层防御、信息边界控制、语义验证以及代理授权保护整合为一个统一的纵深防御模型。我们展示了PISA的参考架构，并在高风险金融应用场景中进行了对抗性评估。实验结果表明，与基线GenAI系统相比，PISA显著降低了提示注入攻击的成功率、幻觉发生率以及未经授权操作的尝试次数。本研究为金融机构提供了一种实用且符合行业特性的方法论，助力其部署安全、可信且符合监管要求的GenAI系统。

title: Prefix-Tuning for Large Language Models Enhanced with Hierarchical Graph Semantics in CI/CD Workflow Generation
authors: Qipeng Yue, Yong Liu, Wenya Fu, Shuai Guo, Yanli Kong, Chengyao Li
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Information Science, Parallel and Distributed Systems (ISPDS)
abstract: In recent years, the automation of continuous integration and deployment in software engineering has been continuously improving. However, the hierarchical complexity of workflows and cross-framework dependencies still limit their efficiency and reliability. Large language models (LLMs) offer new insights into workflow generation, but traditional serialized generation methods struggle to model hierarchical structures and cross-node constraints, often leading to syntactic errors and semantic mismatches. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a hierarchical graph semantic-enhanced prefix finetuning method for workflow generation. First, structural features of workflows are extracted through Syntax Trees (ASTs), forming data representations for fine-tuning. Next, a Transformer is used for semantic mapping and feature encoding, establishing a unified representation of structure and semantics. Finally, a hierarchical graph neural network generates prefix vectors and injects them into the attention layers of a large language model, enabling parameter-efficient, structure-guided generation. Additionally, to avoid sequence bottlenecks and long-dependency attenuation, a hierarchical attention mechanism is employed to enhance the modeling capability of the graph neural network, ensuring structural consistency and semantic integrity throughout the decoding process. Experimental results show that this method effectively alleviates issues of structural and semantic inconsistency, demonstrating its effectiveness and universality in workflow generation tasks.
title_zh: 基于层次图语义增强的前缀调优在CI/CD工作流生成中的应用
abstract_zh: 近年来，软件工程中的持续集成与部署自动化水平持续提升。然而，工作流的层级复杂性以及跨框架依赖关系仍然制约着其效率与可靠性。大型语言模型（LLMs）为工作流生成提供了新的思路，但传统的序列化生成方法难以有效建模层级结构和跨节点约束，常导致语法错误和语义不一致。为解决上述问题，本文提出一种基于层次图语义增强前缀微调的工作流生成方法。首先，通过抽象语法树（AST）提取工作流的结构特征，构建用于微调的数据表示；其次，利用Transformer进行语义映射与特征编码，建立结构与语义的统一表征；最后，采用层次图神经网络生成前缀向量，并将其注入大型语言模型的注意力层，实现参数高效、结构引导的生成过程。此外，为避免序列瓶颈及长距离依赖衰减问题，引入层次化注意力机制，增强图神经网络的建模能力，确保解码过程中结构一致性与语义完整性。实验结果表明，该方法有效缓解了结构与语义不一致的问题，在工作流生成任务中展现出良好的有效性与通用性。

title: Optimizing System Performance for Autonomous Recovery in AI-Native Networks Using Large Language Models
authors: Liming Huang, Yulei Wu, Dimitra Simeonidou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: Future networks are expected to be AI-native, where AI is no longer an external tool but an integral element of the network infrastructure, with a complete AI lifecycle management. This lifecycle spans data collection, model training, inference, and evaluation, and must be coordinated alongside network infrastructure and application performance to maintain reliable end-to-end system behavior. However, such deep integration introduces new challenges, particularly in managing performance degradation caused by complex dependencies across lifecycle components in the system level. Traditional optimization methods, which isolate AI components as well as their interactions with networks and applications, fail to provide system-level assurance in AI-native environments. To address this, we propose a system-level performance optimization framework based on Large Language Models (LLMs) as autonomous reasoning agents within an Agentic AI paradigm. Our approach introduces a two-phase learning strategy: (1) fine-tuning the LLM with knowledge from AI solutions, networks, and applications to enable autonomous identification of performance-affecting factors, especially performance bottlenecks; and (2) real-world refinement using interaction-based learning and a reward-driven alignment mechanism that ties LLM decisions to application performance improvements. By embedding embodied intelligence into the network operation, the framework enables adaptive and lifecycle-aware performance recovery. We validate our method on a real-time 3D reconstruction task within an AI-native network, achieving a 94.5% performance recovery rate with minimal correction latency. The results demonstrate the effectiveness of LLM-powered agentic reasoning and adaptation in maintaining system performance and mitigating performance degradation in complex AI-native networking scenarios.
title_zh: 基于大语言模型的AI原生网络中自主恢复系统的性能优化
abstract_zh: 未来网络预计将实现AI原生化，即AI不再作为外部工具存在，而是成为网络基础设施的内在组成部分，并具备完整的AI生命周期管理能力。这一生命周期涵盖数据采集、模型训练、推理与评估等环节，必须与网络基础设施及应用性能协同管理，以确保端到端系统的可靠运行。然而，这种深度集成也带来了新的挑战，尤其是在系统层面管理由生命周期各组件间复杂依赖关系引发的性能退化问题。传统的优化方法往往将AI组件及其与网络、应用的交互关系孤立处理，难以在AI原生环境中提供系统级的保障。为此，我们提出一种基于大语言模型（LLM）的系统级性能优化框架，该框架以“智能体AI”范式为基础，将LLM作为自主推理智能体。我们的方法采用两阶段学习策略：（1）利用AI解决方案、网络和应用领域的知识对LLM进行微调，使其能够自主识别影响性能的关键因素，尤其是性能瓶颈；（2）通过基于交互的学习方式与奖励驱动的对齐机制，在真实环境中持续优化LLM决策，使其与应用性能提升紧密关联。通过将具身智能嵌入网络运维过程，该框架实现了自适应且具备生命周期感知的性能恢复能力。我们在一个AI原生网络中的实时3D重建任务上验证了该方法，实现了94.5%的性能恢复率，且校正延迟极低。实验结果表明，基于LLM的智能体推理与自适应机制在维持系统性能、缓解复杂AI原生网络场景下性能退化方面具有显著有效性。

title: Policy-Bound, Verifier-Pluggable Smart Contract Framework for Auditable Healthcare Analytics
authors: Muhammad Saeed Javed, Ali Hennache, Muhammad Imran, Saddam Hussain Abbasi
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Healthcare analytics involving sensitive patient data demand robust statistical safeguards alongside verifiable compliance mechanisms that allow regulators to independently reconstruct query operations, applied policies, and reported outcomes without accessing raw medical information.We present a governance-first, upgradeable on-chain framework that anchors complete request lifecycles to public blockchain using keccak256 cryptographic commitments. This approach delineates a process wherein researchers submit structured queries, a controller captures active policy states, and hospitals provide result digests. A comprehensive and immutable event trail for this entire process is maintained on the Ethereum Sepolia blockchain. External auditors can then reconstruct complete timelines and verify consistent binding between requests, policies, and outcomes. The proposed modular verifier interface, currently implemented via configurable MockVerifier, maintains stable Application Binary Interface compatibility for future production verifier integration while validating end-to-end governance. We demonstrate the framework’s practicality through a detailed diabetes prevalence analytics case study. We provide detailed gas consumption profiles, request latency measurements, and observable failure modes that demonstrate how governance rules translate into enforceable reversions. The architecture maintains minimal on-chain state with Layer-2 readiness, offering immediate regulatory accountability while preserving straightforward upgrade paths to advanced cryptographic components like zk verifiers (Groth16), verifiable differential privacy through VRF-seeded randomness, content-addressed artifact storage, and versioned policy management. This approach effectively separates initial deployment feasibility from computationally intensive cryptography while delivering immediately actionable, externally verifiable evidence of policy compliance.
title_zh: 可审计医疗数据分析的政策约束、验证器可插拔智能合约框架
abstract_zh: 涉及敏感患者数据的医疗健康分析，需要强有力的统计保护措施，以及可验证的合规机制，使监管机构能够在不接触原始医疗信息的情况下，独立重建查询操作、应用的政策及报告结果。我们提出一种以治理为先、可升级的链上框架，通过keccak256加密承诺将完整的请求生命周期锚定在公共区块链上。该方法定义了一个流程：研究人员提交结构化查询，控制器捕获当前的政策状态，医院提供结果摘要。整个过程的全面且不可篡改的事件记录被保存在以太坊Sepolia测试链上。外部审计人员随后可重建完整的事件时间线，并验证请求、政策与结果之间的一致性绑定关系。 所提出的模块化验证器接口，当前通过可配置的MockVerifier实现，保持了稳定的应用二进制接口（ABI）兼容性，为未来生产环境验证器的集成提供了支持，同时确保端到端治理的有效性。我们通过一个详尽的糖尿病患病率分析案例研究，展示了该框架的实际可行性。研究提供了详细的Gas消耗分析、请求延迟测量以及可观测的故障模式，清晰展示了治理规则如何转化为可执行的回滚机制。 该架构在链上保持最小状态，具备Layer-2扩展能力，既实现了即时的监管问责，又保留了向先进密码学组件（如zk验证器Groth16）、基于VRF种子随机性的可验证差分隐私、内容寻址的成果存储以及版本化政策管理等高级功能平滑升级的路径。该方案有效分离了初始部署的可行性与计算密集型密码学的实现，同时立即提供可被外部验证的、具有法律效力的合规证据。

title: SOCot: Find Shorter Optimal Chain of Thought Using DPO Training
authors: Kaiyi Wang, Chen Dong, Feiran Yuan
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Machine Learning and Natural Language Processing (MLNLP)
abstract: Reasoning is a fundamental ability of large language models (LLMs), and many studies have focused on enhancing the model's reasoning ability by generating long chain of thought (CoT). However, the longer intermediate reasoning steps result in longer output sequences, which increases the computational cost of the inference and limits the model's applicability in delay-sensitive practical scenarios. In this paper, we propose SOCoT, a reinforcement learning (RL) optimization method based on direct preference optimization (DPO) for training LLM to reduce the length of CoT without affecting its reasoning ability. SOCoT uses on-policy sampling to obtain pairwise training data and provides real-time preferences based on output length and correctness. Penalties are also imposed for incorrect output. We conduct sufficient experiments to evaluate the effectiveness and efficiency of SOCoT and results show it outperforms the existing state-of-the-art methods. In addition, SOCoT can be applied directly to any LLM and reduces the output length while maintaining model accuracy.
title_zh: SOCot：使用DPO训练寻找更短的最优思维链
abstract_zh: 推理是大型语言模型（LLMs）的一项基本能力，许多研究致力于通过生成较长的思维链（Chain-of-Thought, CoT）来提升模型的推理能力。然而，更长的中间推理步骤会导致输出序列变长，从而增加推理过程的计算开销，限制了模型在对延迟敏感的实际应用场景中的适用性。本文提出SOCoT，一种基于直接偏好优化（DPO）的强化学习（RL）优化方法，用于训练LLM在不损害推理能力的前提下缩短思维链的长度。SOCoT采用在线策略采样获取成对训练数据，并根据输出长度和正确性提供实时偏好信号，同时对错误输出施加惩罚。我们进行了充分的实验以评估SOCoT的有效性和效率，结果表明其优于现有的最先进方法。此外，SOCoT可直接应用于任意LLM，在保持模型准确率的同时显著减少输出长度。

title: Two-Stage Fine-Tuning for Retrieval-Augmented Large Language Models via Retriever and Prompt Optimization
authors: Xiaochen Lai, Xiaodi Pan, Yongkang Su, Genglin Zhang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Robotics, and Communication (ICAIRC)
abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) effectively enhances large language models (LLMs) with external knowledge for more factual and accurate outputs. However, existing RAG methods often rely on manually crafted prompts or full fine-tuning, which can impair LLM generalization and increase computational costs. To provide a more parameter-efficient and empirically grounded alternative, we adopt a two-stage fine-tuning strategy that improves RAG efficiency while helping to preserve LLM capabilities. In the first stage, the retriever is fully fine-tuned to boost retrieval precision. In the second stage, a prompt-tuning module is introduced for the generator, inserting trainable virtual tokens between retrieved documents and the query to guide knowledge utilization without altering model parameters. Using E5-Base as the retriever and LLaMA-2-7B as the generator, our method is trained on MS MARCO and evaluated on five QA benchmarks. Experiments show consistent gains in EM and F1, confirming the practical effectiveness, generalizability, and parameter efficiency of our approach. While conceptually simple, this framework empirically demonstrates the strong synergy between retriever fine-tuning and prompt-based generator adaptation, offering a scalable, plug-and-play recipe for RAG enhancement.
title_zh: 基于检索器与提示优化的两阶段微调方法用于增强检索的大语言模型
abstract_zh: 检索增强生成（RAG）通过引入外部知识，有效提升了大型语言模型（LLMs）输出的事实性与准确性。然而，现有的RAG方法通常依赖于人工设计的提示（prompt）或全量微调，这不仅可能损害LLM的泛化能力，还显著增加了计算成本。为提供一种更参数高效且基于实证的替代方案，我们采用了一种两阶段微调策略，在提升RAG效率的同时，有助于保持LLM的原有能力。在第一阶段，对检索器进行全量微调，以提高检索的精确度；在第二阶段，为生成器引入提示微调（prompt-tuning）模块，通过在检索到的文档与查询之间插入可训练的虚拟标记（virtual tokens），引导模型有效利用知识，而无需修改模型本身的参数。我们以E5-Base作为检索器，LLaMA-2-7B作为生成器，在MS MARCO数据集上进行训练，并在五个问答（QA）基准上进行评估。实验结果表明，该方法在EM和F1指标上均实现了稳定提升，验证了所提方法在实际应用中的有效性、泛化能力以及参数高效性。尽管该框架在概念上较为简单，但实证结果充分展示了检索器微调与基于提示的生成器适配之间强大的协同效应，为RAG性能提升提供了一种可扩展、即插即用的实用方案。

title: Toward Low-SWaP Cognitive Agents: Neuromorphic Intelligence and FPGA-Based Deployments of Event Neural Networks
authors: Trevor Bihl, Rajashree Majumder, Zhewei Wang, Avinash Karanth, Jundong Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Traditional artificial intelligence (AI) solutions are often high-SWaP (Size, Weight, and Power), data hungry, and lack the efficiency, resilience, and autonomy exhibited by biological intelligence. As a consequence, autonomous and cognitive agents suffer from various technical bottlenecks precluding their widespread use. Neuromorphic computing offers the potential to develop learning machines that perceive, adapt, and learn continually. In this paper, we survey the state-of-the-art in neuromorphic intelligence, including advances in spiking neural networks (SNNs), emerging brain-inspired hardware platforms, supporting software stacks, and representative application domains. Key to these advances are deployment pipelines for development, evaluation, and testing of solutions. Thus, in parallel, we further present a preliminary FPGA-based deployment pipeline for both artificial neural networks (ANNs) and SNNs. This approach not only provides a practical low-SWaP alternative for edge AI but also enables head-to-head benchmarking of neuromorphic solutions against conventional neural models on reconfigurable hardware. Together, these perspectives define a roadmap toward robust, low-power cognitive systems capable of realworld autonomy across embedded and constrained environments.
title_zh: 面向低SWaP认知智能体：神经形态智能与基于FPGA的事件神经网络部署
abstract_zh: 传统的人工智能（AI）解决方案通常具有高SWaP（尺寸、重量和功耗）特征，数据需求量大，且缺乏生物智能所表现出的高效性、鲁棒性和自主性。因此，自主与认知代理在实际应用中面临诸多技术瓶颈，限制了其广泛应用。神经形态计算为开发能够持续感知、适应和学习的智能机器提供了可能。本文综述了神经形态智能领域的最新进展，涵盖脉冲神经网络（SNNs）的突破、新兴类脑硬件平台、配套软件栈以及代表性应用领域。这些进展的关键在于开发、评估和测试解决方案的部署流程。为此，本文还提出了一种基于FPGA的初步部署流程，适用于人工神经网络（ANNs）和SNNs。该方法不仅为边缘AI提供了一种实用的低SWaP替代方案，还能够在可重构硬件上实现神经形态解决方案与传统神经模型的直接对比基准测试。综上所述，这些视角共同勾勒出一条通往鲁棒、低功耗认知系统的发展路线，使智能系统能够在嵌入式及资源受限环境中实现真正的现实世界自主运行。

title: Generative AI for IoT Security: A Review of Attack Detection, Prevention, and Secure Data Transmission Mechanisms
authors: Kanchan Vishalkumar Wankhade, Aditi Sharma
publish: 2025 1st IEEE Uttar Pradesh Section Women in Engineering International Conference on Electrical Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPWIECON)
abstract: The Internet of Things' (IoT) explosive expansion has created both enormous benefits and serious cybersecurity dangers. Among them, distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) assaults continue to be a maj or worry since hackers frequently take advantage of weak IoT devices to interfere with networks. These developing risks are posing a growing threat to traditional security solutions, particularly because IoT devices have limited resources, making advanced protection challenging to install. Generative AI has shown promise as a way to close this gap. Beyond the limits of traditional methods, techniques like Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) and Variational Autoencoders (VAEs) provide more accurate anomaly detection and proactive threat mitigation. With a focus on identifying and stopping threats while guaranteeing secure data transmission, this study explores how generative AI might improve IoT security. IoT networks can strengthen their defences against complex and widespread cyberattacks by utilising these cutting-edge models.
title_zh: 生成式人工智能在物联网安全中的应用：攻击检测、预防及安全数据传输机制综述
abstract_zh: 物联网（IoT）的爆炸式增长带来了巨大的收益，同时也引发了严重的网络安全威胁。其中，分布式拒绝服务（DDoS）攻击持续成为一大隐患，因为黑客经常利用安全薄弱的物联网设备来干扰网络运行。这些不断演化的风险正对传统安全解决方案构成日益严峻的挑战，尤其是由于物联网设备资源有限，难以部署高级防护措施。生成式人工智能（Generative AI）展现出弥补这一差距的巨大潜力。与传统方法相比，生成对抗网络（GANs）和变分自编码器（VAEs）等技术能够实现更精准的异常检测和主动威胁缓解。本研究聚焦于识别与阻止网络威胁，同时确保数据传输的安全性，探讨生成式人工智能如何提升物联网安全防护能力。通过应用这些前沿模型，物联网网络能够增强防御能力，有效应对复杂且大规模的网络攻击。

title: Synthesizing Specialized Sparse Tensor Accelerators for FPGAs via High-Level Functional Abstractions
authors: Hamza Javed, Christophe Dubach
publish: 2026 IEEE/ACM International Symposium on Code Generation and Optimization (CGO)
abstract: Sparsity is inherent in many applications such as machine learning and graph analytics. However, achieving high efficiency in sparse computations requires specialized hardware accelerators like FPGAs, as traditional accelerators typically cater to dense data. While high level synthesis enables the automatic generation of FPGA-based accelerators, generic solutions produced via C-based synthesis flows often demand extensive development time, leading designers to prioritize broad applicability over fine-grained structural specialization. Consequently, these accelerators fail to fully exploit FPGA’s reconfigurablility, leaving substantial performance and efficiency gains untapped.This paper pushes the boundary by automatically generating specialized accelerators that match a given fixed sparse structure (e.g., in static graph analytics and pruned neural networks). It achieves this by leveraging functional abstractions within high level synthesis, an approach that has already proven effective in automating the generation of specialized dense tensor accelerator. Tensor shapes are encoded directly in the type system and specialized primitives for irregular data are introduced. Together, these innovations enable a concise specification of sparse accelerators and drive advanced optimizations—including dynamic partitioning and vector sharding—to produce hardware precisely tailored to the sparsity pattern of the underlying tensors.Compared to state-of-the-art generic accelerators (HiSparse, HiSpMV and GraphLily), the approach achieves up to a 2.8× improvement in bandwidth efficiency for sparse matrix computations and a 1.8× speedup on graph algorithms. Against the hls4ml neural network acceleration framework, it achieves up to a 1.8× improvement in throughput with a 4× reduction in resource usage, enabling scaling to larger networks. These results establish this approach as a flexible, powerful, and rapid solution for designing high-performance specialized sparse accelerators.
title_zh: 通过高层次功能抽象实现FPGA专用稀疏张量加速器的综合
abstract_zh:
稀疏性在许多应用中普遍存在，例如机器学习和图分析。然而，要实现稀疏计算的高效处理，通常需要专用的硬件加速器（如FPGA），因为传统加速器主要针对密集数据设计。尽管高层次综合（HLS）能够自动生成基于FPGA的加速器，但通过基于C语言的综合流程生成的通用解决方案往往需要大量开发时间，导致设计者更倾向于追求广泛的适用性，而非细粒度的结构优化。结果，这些加速器未能充分挖掘FPGA的可重构性优势，导致大量性能和能效提升潜力未被释放。

本文通过自动构建与给定固定稀疏结构（如静态图分析和剪枝神经网络中的结构）相匹配的专用加速器，突破了现有技术的局限。该方法利用高层次综合中的功能抽象，这一思路已在自动化生成专用密集张量加速器方面证明了其有效性。本文将张量形状直接编码在类型系统中，并引入用于处理不规则数据的专用原语。这些创新共同实现了对稀疏加速器的简洁描述，并推动了高级优化技术（如动态分区和向量分片）的实现，从而生成与底层张量稀疏模式精确匹配的硬件。

与当前最先进的通用加速器（HiSparse、HiSpMV 和 GraphLily）相比，该方法在稀疏矩阵计算中实现了高达2.8倍的带宽效率提升，在图算法上实现了1.8倍的加速。与hls4ml神经网络加速框架相比，该方法在吞吐量上最高提升1.8倍，同时资源使用量减少4倍，从而支持更大规模网络的扩展。这些结果表明，该方法是一种灵活、强大且快速的设计高性能专用稀疏加速器的解决方案。

title: Graphene: Leveraging Transformers with Control Flow Modalities for Malware Detection
authors: Andrew Wheeler, Kshitiz Aryal, Maanak Gupta
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on AI in Cybersecurity (ICAIC)
abstract: The proliferation of malicious programs for the Windows operating system has highlighted the need for detection frameworks that protect these vital systems. Traditional malware detection approaches struggle to generalize across the diverse and evolving behavioral patterns that are demonstrated by modern malware. In order to gain an upper hand against malware developers, researchers have experimented with varying representations of programs that can be used in conjunction with machine learning models to automatically detect and mitigate malware threats.This work presents Graphene, a graph-based malware detection framework that leverages a RoBERTa-based classification model for malware detection. Graphene encodes program behavior through graph representations that are subsequently linearized into function-call sequences, enabling the use of advanced language-model architectures for malware detection. We evaluate Graphene on a dataset of approximately 150,000 Windows Portable Execubable (PE) files and benchmark its performance against two conventional machine learning models: a deep neural network (DNN) and a recurrent neural network (RNN). Experimental results demonstrate that Graphene significantly outperforms these baselines, achieving 94% accuracy with a false positive rate below 4%. These findings show the effectiveness of transformer-based architectures, when informed by graph representations, in advancing the state of the art automated malware detection.
title_zh: 石墨烯：利用带有控制流模态的Transformer进行恶意软件检测
abstract_zh: 针对Windows操作系统的恶意程序泛滥，凸显了构建能够保护这些关键系统的检测框架的迫切需求。传统的恶意软件检测方法难以在现代恶意软件所表现出的多样化且不断演变的行为模式之间实现有效泛化。为了在与恶意软件开发者的技术对抗中占据优势，研究人员尝试采用多种程序表示方法，结合机器学习模型，以实现恶意软件威胁的自动检测与缓解。本文提出了一种名为Graphene的基于图的恶意软件检测框架，该框架利用基于RoBERTa的分类模型进行恶意软件检测。Graphene通过图结构表示程序行为，并将这些图线性化为函数调用序列，从而使得先进的语言模型架构能够应用于恶意软件检测任务。我们在约15万份Windows可移植可执行文件（PE文件）的数据集上对Graphene进行了评估，并将其性能与两种传统机器学习模型——深度神经网络（DNN）和循环神经网络（RNN）——进行了对比。实验结果表明，Graphene显著优于这些基线模型，达到了94%的准确率，且误报率低于4%。这些发现表明，当结合图结构表示时，基于Transformer的架构在推动自动化恶意软件检测技术发展方面具有显著优势，能够有效提升当前技术水平。

title: From Threads to Tiles: T2T, a Compiler for CUDA-to-NPU Translation via 2D Vectorization
authors: Shuaijiang Li, Jiacheng Zhao, Ying Liu, Shuoming Zhang, Lei Chen, Yijin Li, Yangyu Zhang, Zhicheng Li, Runyu Zhou, Xiyu Shi, Chunwei Xia, Yuan Wen, Xiaobing Feng, Huimin Cui
publish: 2026 IEEE/ACM International Symposium on Code Generation and Optimization (CGO)
abstract: CUDA’s programming model, exposing massive parallelism via fine-grained scalar threads, has become the de facto standard for GPU computing. Concurrently, NPUs are emerging as highly efficient accelerators, but their architecture is fundamentally different, relying on coarse-grained, explicit 2-D tile-based instructions. This creates a critical challenge: bridging the semantic gap "From Threads to Tiles". A direct translation is infeasible, as it requires lifting the implicit parallelism of CUDA’s scalar model into the explicit, multi-dimensional vector space of NPUs, a problem we formalize as a lifting challenge.This paper introduces T2T, a compiler framework that automates this "Threads to Tiles" translation via the 2-D Vectorization technique. T2T first transforms a CUDA kernel’s implicit SIMT parallelism into a structured, explicit loop nest via our Unified Parallelism Abstraction (UPA), making the parallelism analyzable. From this representation, T2T’s core vectorization engine systematically selects optimal pairs of loops and maps them onto the NPU’s 2-D tile instructions to maximize hardware utilization. To ensure correctness and handle performance-critical CUDA features, a final set of semantics-preserving optimizations is applied, including efficient control-flow management and vectorization of warp-level intrinsics.We implement T2T based on Polygeist and evaluate representative NPU architectures. On a diverse set of benchmarks, kernels translated by T2T achieve up to 73% of native CUDA performance on an A100 GPU and outperform baseline translation approaches by up to 6.9×. Our work demonstrates that a systematic, compiler-driven approach to 2-D vectorization is a principled and high-performance path for porting the rich CUDA ecosystem to the evolving landscape of NPU accelerators.
title_zh: 从线程到瓷砖：通过二维向量化实现CUDA到NPU翻译的T2T编译器
abstract_zh:
CUDA 的编程模型通过细粒度的标量线程暴露大规模并行性，已成为 GPU 计算的事实标准。与此同时，神经网络处理单元（NPUs）正作为高效加速器崭露头角，但其架构本质不同，依赖粗粒度、显式的二维分块（tile-based）指令。这带来了一个关键挑战：弥合“从线程到分块”的语义鸿沟。直接翻译不可行，因为需要将 CUDA 标量模型中隐含的并行性提升至 NPU 所依赖的显式、多维向量空间，这一问题我们形式化为“提升难题”（lifting challenge）。

本文提出 T2T，一个编译器框架，通过二维向量化（2-D Vectorization）技术自动完成“从线程到分块”的转换。T2T 首先利用其统一并行性抽象（Unified Parallelism Abstraction, UPA），将 CUDA 内核中隐含的 SIMT 并行性转化为结构化、显式的循环嵌套，使并行性可被分析。在此基础上，T2T 的核心向量化引擎系统性地选择最优的循环对，并将其映射到 NPU 的二维分块指令上，以最大化硬件利用率。为确保正确性并处理性能关键的 CUDA 特性，最后还应用一系列保持语义的优化，包括高效的控制流管理以及对 warp 级内建函数的向量化。

我们基于 Polygeist 实现了 T2T，并在多种代表性 NPU 架构上进行了评估。在多样化的基准测试中，T2T 翻译的内核在 A100 GPU 上达到了原生 CUDA 性能的最高 73%，相比基线翻译方法性能提升高达 6.9 倍。我们的工作表明，一种系统化、由编译器驱动的二维向量化方法，是将丰富的 CUDA 生态系统高效移植到不断演进的 NPU 加速器平台上的原则性且高性能的路径。

title: Fine-Grained Multi-modal Fusion for Malware Detection via Cross-Modal Self-Attention
authors: JinXuan Zhang, Wenjie Guo, Jingfeng Xue
publish: 2025 IEEE 13th International Conference on Smart City and Informatization (iSCI)
abstract: Malicious software, as a major security threat in cyberspace, has seen its quantity grow exponentially and the iteration of its variants accelerate. Techniques such as shelling and obfuscation make it highly concealed. Traditional single-modal detection and simple multimodal fusion methods are no longer sufficient to cope with it. To this end, we propose a multimodal fine-grained fusion method for malware detection based on a self-attention mechanism. Binary executables are transformed into grayscale images, from which global and local visual features are extracted using a dual-branch network consisting of ViT and ResNet. In parallel, assembly-level semantic features are obtained by disassembling binaries and encoding function representations with CodeBERT model. Finally, a multi-head self-attention mechanism is employed to achieve fine-grained cross-modal fusion, enabling more precise correlation modeling between binary visual textures and assembly semantics. Experiments on the Microsoft BIG 2015 and VirusShare datasets demonstrate that the proposed method consistently outperforms mainstream models in accuracy and recall, validating its effectiveness.
title_zh: 基于跨模态自注意力的细粒度多模态融合恶意软件检测
abstract_zh: 恶意软件作为网络空间中的主要安全威胁，其数量呈指数级增长，变种迭代速度不断加快。壳技术与混淆技术的应用使其具有极强的隐蔽性。传统的单模态检测方法以及简单的多模态融合手段已难以应对这一挑战。为此，本文提出一种基于自注意力机制的多模态细粒度融合恶意软件检测方法。首先，将二进制可执行文件转换为灰度图像，利用由ViT和ResNet组成的双分支网络提取其全局与局部视觉特征；同时，通过反汇编二进制文件，并使用CodeBERT模型对函数表示进行编码，获取汇编级别的语义特征。最后，采用多头自注意力机制实现细粒度的跨模态融合，从而更精确地建模二进制图像纹理与汇编语义之间的关联关系。在Microsoft BIG 2015和VirusShare数据集上的实验结果表明，所提方法在准确率和召回率方面均持续优于主流模型，验证了其有效性。

title: LLM-VeriOpt: Verification-Guided Reinforcement Learning for LLM-Based Compiler Optimization
authors: Xiangxin Fang, Jiaqin Kang, Rodrigo Rocha, Sam Ainsworth, Lev Mukhanov
publish: 2026 IEEE/ACM International Symposium on Code Generation and Optimization (CGO)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) for compiler optimization have recently emerged as a frontier research direction, with many studies demonstrating their potential to automate and improve low-level code transformations. While various techniques have been proposed to enhance LLMs’ ability to optimize LLVM IR or assembly code, ensuring the semantic equivalence of transformed instructions remains a fundamental prerequisite for safe and effective performance improvement. At the same time, code generated by LLMs is often so far away from being correct that it is very difficult to work out how to improve them to proceed in generating optimizations using the output.In this work, we present LLM-VeriOpt, a novel reinforcement-learning methodology that incorporates feedback from a formal verifier, Alive2, to guide the training of a small-scale model, Qwen-3B. This facilitates the use of Guided Reinforcement via Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO), using semantic-equivalence signals from the Alive2 formal verification tool as part of the reward function. This allows the model to self-correct based on observing and subsequently learning to give correctness feedback during training, giving high code coverage by successfully transforming large amounts of code, while also optimizing it significantly.We demonstrate our technique by designing an LLM-based peephole optimizer over LLVM-IR. Our method significantly improves the correctness of IR optimizations versus the base LLM Qwen-3B applied with just a prompt and no fine-tuning — achieving a 5.4× improvement in code successfully modified. The resulting model produces verifiably correct output 90% of the time, comfortably outperforming larger state-of-the-art LLMs, including Meta’s LLM Compiler. This yields speedups of 2.3× over O0-optimized code, comparable to the handwritten LLVM-instcombine pass, and producing emergent optimizations that outperform it in 20% of cases.
title_zh: LLM-VeriOpt：基于验证引导的强化学习在基于大语言模型的编译器优化中的应用
abstract_zh:
近年来，用于编译器优化的大语言模型（LLMs）已成为前沿研究方向，众多研究展示了其在自动化和提升底层代码转换方面的巨大潜力。尽管已有多种技术被提出以增强LLM在优化LLVM中间表示（IR）或汇编代码方面的能力，但确保转换后指令的语义等价性，仍是实现安全且有效的性能提升的基本前提。与此同时，LLM生成的代码往往距离正确性相去甚远，使得如何改进这些代码以进一步生成优化结果变得极为困难。

在本工作中，我们提出了LLM-VeriOpt，一种新颖的强化学习方法，通过引入形式化验证工具Alive2的反馈来指导小型模型Qwen-3B的训练。该方法采用基于组相对策略优化的引导式强化学习（GRPO），将Alive2形式验证工具提供的语义等价性信号作为奖励函数的一部分。这使得模型能够在训练过程中通过观察并学习如何提供正确性反馈，实现自我修正。该方法在大量代码上实现了高代码覆盖率，同时显著提升了优化效果。

我们通过设计一个基于LLM的LLVM-IR窥孔优化器来验证该技术。与仅使用提示（prompt）而未进行微调的基线LLM Qwen-3B相比，我们的方法在IR优化的正确性方面实现了显著提升——成功修改代码量提高了5.4倍。所得到的模型在90%的情况下生成可验证正确的输出，明显优于更大的前沿LLM，包括Meta的LLM Compiler。该方法在O0优化代码基础上实现了2.3倍的加速，性能与手工编写的LLVM-instcombine优化 pass相当，并在20%的情况下实现了超越该手工优化的新兴优化效果。

title: Multi-Layered Defense Proxy for Home Assistant utilizing Large Language Models
authors: Faran Yazdani, Stephen Antezana, Mohamed Gebril, Sherif Abdelhamid
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on AI in Cybersecurity (ICAIC)
abstract: The ever-increasing integration of large language models, LLMs, in IoT environments necessitates the implementation of robust security measures. Because such systems have access to physical devices, attacks against them can have real-world consequences. Therefore, to address the growing need for security in this realm, we propose a proxy that leverages LLMs to detect prompt injection attempts in a home IoT setting. In this paper, various methods of performing prompt injections on LLMs that have been given access to control physical IoT devices in a home are investigated. We will discuss the relationship between our work and related efforts aimed at securing LLMs, and provide an overview of the contributions this work makes to the field. We will discuss the benefits of our approach and briefly outline potential extensions to our work, both in the realm of IoT devices and in other contexts where LLMs are used.
title_zh: 用于 Home Assistant 的多层防御代理，利用大型语言模型
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）在物联网（IoT）环境中的日益融合，迫切需要实施强有力的安保措施。由于这些系统能够访问物理设备，针对它们的攻击可能带来现实世界中的严重后果。因此，为应对这一领域不断增长的安全需求，本文提出了一种代理机制，利用大型语言模型来检测家庭物联网环境中的提示注入攻击。本文深入研究了在家庭环境中，当大型语言模型被赋予控制物理物联网设备权限时，可能实施的各种提示注入攻击方法。我们将探讨本研究与现有旨在保护大型语言模型安全工作的关联，并概述本研究对相关领域的贡献。此外，本文还将讨论所提方法的优势，并简要展望该工作的潜在扩展方向，涵盖物联网设备领域以及其他大型语言模型应用的场景。
